persona.name: Kai Nguyen

occupation.title: Digital Media Student / Social Media Intern
occupation.organization: University of Oregon / local Portland lifestyle boutique brand
occupation.description: Junior at U of O studying digital media with a social content strategy focus. Works part-time as a social media intern for a small Portland lifestyle brand, managing their TikTok and editing short-form video. Heavily plugged into the creator economy and building a personal beauty-focused TikTok channel on the side.

persona.education: Junior at University of Oregon in Eugene, majoring in Digital Media with a Gender Studies minor. Graduated from Lincoln High School in Portland in 2023. Lives with two roommates in Portland during summers and school breaks.
long_term_goals: Grow their TikTok skincare channel to 100K followers
Work as a brand deal consultant for indie beauty brands
Be visibly non-binary in content creation to normalize gender-fluid beauty
Travel to Vietnam to connect with their heritage and explore Vietnamese beauty traditions
Build financially stable independence without relying on family
persona.style: Kai is expressive, enthusiastic, and hyper-online. They talk fast and reference TikTok trends and creator discourse naturally — 'it's giving glass-skin energy' is an unironic sentence. Very visual communicator: their camera roll is half skincare product shots, half coffee aesthetic. They use they/them pronouns and will gently correct anyone who misses it. Their aesthetic is gender-fluid: e-girl meets soft masc, bold colors, experimental looks. Skin is always a topic of conversation.

traits: Trend-sensitive and socially plugged-in — highly attuned to what's going viral
Enthusiastic and demonstrative; talks about skincare with genuine joy
Impulsive buyer who rationalizes purchases as 'content research'
Inclusive values — advocates for gender-neutral beauty marketing
Short attention span calibrated to TikTok video length
Resilient about skin struggles — turns breakouts into 'healing era' content

interests: TikTok skincare trends and derm-approved viral products
Gender-neutral beauty and inclusive brand marketing
Short-form video content creation and editing (CapCut, Premiere Rush)
Korean skincare — glass skin, slugging, snail mucin
Niche and indie perfume (beginning fragrance nerd)
Studying how algorithms amplify certain beauty content
likes: CeraVe Hydrating Cleanser — reliable drugstore staple
COSRX Snail Mucin Essence (their holy grail for calming reactive skin)
Viral products — first to test whatever Hyram or Doctorly posted this week
Aesthetic skincare packaging that photographs well
Portland indie coffee shops and skincare boutiques
Finding $6 drugstore versions of $60 luxury products
dislikes: Over-exfoliation — learned this the hard way (broke their moisture barrier in 2024)
Brands that don't engage with Gen Z on TikTok — feels out of touch
Heavy synthetic fragrance in any product (irritates their reactive skin)
Influencers who push anything for a paycheck regardless of product quality
Waiting more than 2 days for skincare delivery
Full-coverage matte foundation — not the aesthetic

beliefs: Skincare has no gender — 'for him' and 'for her' labels are outdated and harmful
Brand transparency about ingredients is not optional
Community and representation in beauty matter as much as product performance
Experimentation is how you find what works — impulsive buying is just skincare science
Being visibly non-binary in beauty spaces is an act of community service
skills: Social media analytics and content strategy
Skincare triage after moisture barrier damage (personal experience)
Trend forecasting: spots emerging beauty microtrends on TikTok 2–3 weeks early
Budget-stretching: makes drugstore routines look luxury on camera

general: Buys at least one new skincare product per week (usually under $20)
Films 'first impression' TikToks for every new product they try
Their bathroom shelf rotates constantly — few products last longer than 3 months
Follows 40+ skincare accounts and checks the For You Page every morning
Broke their skin barrier in 2024 from daily AHA/BHA use; now more cautious but still experimental

morning: CeraVe Hydrating Cleanser, COSRX Snail Mucin, lightweight moisturizer
Purito mineral SPF 50 (switched for a cleaner look on film)
Gender-fluid outfit assembly for internship or class
workday: Morning: internship at Portland boutique brand, editing TikTok content
Afternoon: digital marketing and social media theory classes
Constant TikTok monitoring between tasks for trend spotting
evening: Micellar water + gentle foam double cleanse
Snail mucin essence, barrier-repair moisturizer, occasional Vaseline slug
Films product review content 2–3x per week
weekend: Saturday: pop-up market or indie store skincare haul in Portland
Batch-film TikTok content (3–4 videos in one session)
Sunday: long barrier-repair routine + sheet mask + journaling
Social: LGBTQ+ community events or chill hangouts with roommates

health.physical: Reactive, sensitized skin recovering from a moisture-barrier breach caused by daily AHA/BHA overuse in 2024. Now managing with gentle ceramide-focused products. Generally healthy; cycles to campus and practices yoga for stress management.
health.mental: Generally positive but navigates social anxiety in non-affirming spaces. Portland has been a supportive environment for their identity. Journaling and community help. Occasionally anxious about finances and career uncertainty.

relationships.name: Linh Nguyen
relationships.description: Immigrated from Vietnam in 2001; runs a nail salon in Portland. Warm relationship with Kai even though she doesn't fully understand non-binary identity. She used to buy them drugstore skincare as a teenager.

relationships.name: River
relationships.description: Non-binary classmate at U of O, also in digital media. They film content together and swap skincare products constantly. River introduced Kai to COSRX snail mucin.

relationships.name: Sam
relationships.description: 23-year-old barista and musician Kai has been seeing casually for 4 months. Sam has zero skincare knowledge but finds Kai's dedication to it endearing.
